source_intake_record.raw_question_text: A square coil of wire of side length 12 cm consists of 8 insulated turns. The coil is stationary in a uniform magnetic field. The plane of the coil is perpendicular to the magnetic field, as shown in Fig. 6.1. The flux density $B$ of the magnetic field varies with time $t$ as shown in Fig. 6.2. Calculate the magnitude of $E$.
extraction_notes: image entries are PIL object representations rather than explicit file paths or URLs

image_dependency: question_text explicitly refers to 'Fig. 6.1' and 'Fig. 6.2' for the setup and the time-varying magnetic field
requires_image=true and text_dominant=false indicate the figures are central rather than optional
the needed magnetic-field-versus-time information appears to come from the plotted figure, not fully from text alone
asset_registry_record shows 2 existing images and registry_passed=true
multi_step: solving requires combining geometry of the square coil (side length to area) with number of turns and electromagnetic induction
the task likely needs extracting a rate of change from the B vs t graph and then applying Faraday's law
there is at least a two-stage reasoning chain: read graph data, then compute induced emf with units
verifiability: answer_type is short_text and answer_text gives a single numeric result: '0.077 V'
the physics formula and intermediate quantities are structured enough for rule-based or worked-solution checking
registry_passed=true, question_present=true, and answer_present=true support reliable downstream evaluation
verifiability is slightly reduced because graph-reading may introduce tolerance in the extracted slope

decision_reasons: 题干、答案和两张图片资产齐全，且 registry_passed=true、issues 为空，核心语义支撑充分
综合评分较高：image_dependency_score=0.91、multi_step_score=0.76、verifiability_score=0.84，符合进入候选池条件
虽然存在 graph-reading-tolerance 和 image-dependent-quantitative-readout 风险，但这属于可验证的定量读图误差，不构成核心语义缺失
答案为明确的短文本数值结果（0.077 V），仍具备较好的下游核验可行性
